messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Agents: Open in Editor should restore Agent Host chat session
ISSUE BODY: ### Repro steps

1. Start or open an Agent Host session with a worktree in the Agents window.
2. Select **Open in Editor**.

### Actual

VS Code opens the session worktree in a new Editor window, but does not open the corresponding Agent Host chat session.

### Expected

The Editor window should open both the worktree and the corresponding chat session, matching the extension-host Copilot CLI behavior.

### Proposed fix

Pass the session resource through the existing VS Code protocol URL handoff. The main process already forwards its `session` parameter as `vscode:openChatSession` to the target Editor window.

Draft fix: [#323316](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/323316)

(Written by Copilot)

--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Instructions  
1. **Always read the `sessions` skill first.** This is your primary source of truth for the sessions architecture.
- Invoke `skill: "sessions"`.
2. Focus your work on `src/vs/sessions/`.
3. Avoid making changes to core VS Code files (`src/vs/workbench/`, `src/vs/platform/`, etc.) unless absolutely necessary for the agents window functionality.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\instructions\agentHostTesting.instructions.md =====  
--- description: Architecture documentation for VS Code AI Customization view. Use when working in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization` applyTo: 'src/vs/platform/agentHost/**'
---

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent Host  
The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory.  
If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:///<path/to/agent-host-protocol>/plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent host system-prompt customization  
This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host**
(ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's
`extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Copilot CLI Integration  
This folder contains the Copilot CLI integration for VS Code Chat. It enables users to open a new Chat window and interact with a Copilot CLI agent instance directly within VS Code. **VS Code provides the UI, Copilot CLI SDK provides the smarts.**  
> **Important:** The Copilot CLI agent functionality is powered by the `@github/copilot/sdk` package. See the SDK package for full type definitions.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## End to End Testing  
You can run `node ./scripts/code-agent-host.js` to start an agent host. If you pass `--enable-mock-agent`, then the `ScriptedMockAgent` will be used.  
By default this will listen on `ws://127.0.0.1:8081`. You can then use the `ahp-websocket` client, when available, to connect to and communicate with it.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\instructions\ai-customization.instructions.md =====  
--- description: Architecture documentation for VS Code AI Customization view. Use when working in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization` applyTo: 'src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/**'
---

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Agents: Open in Editor should restore Agent Host chat session",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "chat", "protocol", "feature"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As an Agent Host user, I want the 'Open in Editor' action to open both the worktree and the corresponding chat session so that I can continue my agent conversation without switching windows.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given an Agent Host session is active with a worktree, When clicking 'Open in Editor', Then VS Code opens a new editor window containing the worktree AND a new chat tab showing the same session",
    "The chat session opened should be identical to what would be shown if opening via Copilot CLI directly",
    "The protocol URL must include the vscode:openChatSession parameter with the correct session resource identifier",
    "Existing 'Open in Editor' functionality for non-Agent Host sessions remains unchanged"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Review existing vscode:openChatSession protocol implementation to understand required parameters and format",
      "Identify where the session resource is available when 'Open in Editor' action is triggered for Agent Host sessions",
      "Modify the Agent Host 'Open in Editor' action handler to construct a protocol URL including the chat session parameter",
      "Ensure proper error handling if the session cannot be retrieved or protocol fails"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Verify that the new chat tab opens correctly when 'Open in Editor' is clicked for Agent Host sessions",
      "Confirm the chat UI displays the correct conversation history matching the agent host session"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test case: Open Agent Host session, click 'Open in Editor', verify both editor and chat tabs open with same session content",
      "Test case: Verify existing non-Agent-Host 'Open in Editor' functionality is not affected",
      "Test case: Test error scenarios when Agent Host is unavailable or session cannot be retrieved"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed by team lead",
    "All unit tests passing for modified action handlers",
    "Integration test verifying chat session restoration works end-to-end",
    "Documentation updated to reflect new 'Open in Editor' behavior for Agent Host"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the exact format of the vscode:openChatSession protocol URL parameter that needs to be passed?",
    "Is there an existing utility function in src/vs/sessions/ that constructs these protocol URLs we can reuse?",
    "Should this change affect how other Agent Host actions handle session restoration?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The session object contains both worktree and chat session references",
    "The existing vscode:openChatSession mechanism is stable and doesn't need changes",
    "Agent Host sessions have a unique identifier that can be used in the protocol URL"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent host system-prompt customization",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Copilot CLI Integration documentation",
    "D:\\VSss-codedata\\vscode\\.github\\instructions\\agentHostTesting.instructions.md"
  ]
}
```
